title: Segfault: unchecked `PyUnicode_AsUTF8` NULL deref in `pegen.c:33`
description: Tokenizing a line that contains a non-ASCII character calls _get_col_offsets (Python/Python-tokenize.c) -> _PyPegen_byte_offset_to_character_offset_line(line, ...). That function does `data = PyUnicode_AsUTF8(line)` (pegen.c:29) and then indexes `data[col_offset]` (pegen.c:33) without checking data for NULL. Under allocation failure PyUnicode_AsUTF8 cannot build/cache the UTF-8 form and returns NULL, so data[col_offset] dereferences NULL -> SIGSEGV on all build configurations. The non-ASCII char is required: _get_col_offsets only runs when a token's byte offset differs from its character offset.
notes: Clean, fully root-caused unchecked-allocation NULL deref. `line` is a valid unicode (gdb-confirmed); only data==NULL. Deterministic minimal stdlib repro (4/4): `list(tokenize.generate_tokens(io.StringIO('ü\n').readline))` under the set_nomemory sweep -> segv at pegen.c:33 on all four builds. Found by the LOCAL fleet via the NEW Phase-2 class fuzzing: an `oc5` CONSTRUCTOR sweep of `_pyrepl._module_completer.ImportParser`, which tokenizes import text -> the C tokenizer column-offset path. First bug attributable to OOM Phase 2 (constructor/method coverage). Distinct from the other pegen bugs OOM-0019 (pegen_errors.c raise_error_known_location) and OOM-0021 (pegen.c _PyPegen_run_parser / set_syntax_error_metadata) -- different function, segv vs abort/fatal. Fix: NULL-check the PyUnicode_AsUTF8 result in _PyPegen_byte_offset_to_character_offset_line and propagate the error (or have _get_col_offsets handle it).
